events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: shaft alignment in progress, coupling gap out of spec. Need to evaluate procedure sequence and state what must happen before work can continue.

Coupling gap out of spec means the alignment is not within tolerance. Work is in-progress. This is a sequence violation if they're proceeding with alignment while coupling gap is out of spec, or if they haven't addressed soft foot, pipe strain, etc. before alignment.

Per API 686, before shaft alignment: check soft foot, pipe strain, baseplate grouting, foundation, shimming, etc. Coupling gap out of spec indicates alignment not complete or defective component.

Decision: fail because non-compliant. Risk: medium-high. Component condition: installed-defective (coupling gap out of spec).

Actions: stop, verify soft foot, check pipe strain, re-measure, correct shimming, recheck.

Let me write the JSON.

  "rationale": "An out-of-spec coupling gap during in-progress alignment indicates the prerequisite checks (soft foot, pipe strain, shimming, gap setting) have not been satisfactorily completed. Per API 686, these conditions must be resolved before final alignment can proceed; continuing would embed misalignment and risk equipment damage.",
